Class AbstractAiProvider

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.event.BasicPropertyChangeSource
uno.anahata.asi.agi.provider.AbstractAiProvider
All Implemented Interfaces:
PropertyChangeSource, Rebindable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AnthropicProvider, GeminiAiProvider, OpenAiChatCompletionsProvider, OpenAiResponsesProvider
public abstract class AbstractAiProvider extends BasicPropertyChangeSource
The abstract base class for all AI model providers, now with model caching. Its primary responsibilities are to discover available models and manage API keys.
Author:
anahata

    Constructor
    Description

    No-arg constructor required for Kryo serialization and dynamic instantiation.

    Constructs a new provider instance with a specific UUID.

    getPropertyChangeSupport

  • Field Details

    • allowedModels

      private List<String> allowedModels
      An optional whitelist of model IDs. If not empty, only models matching IDs in this list will be returned by getModels().

    • asiContainer

      private transient AbstractAsiContainer asiContainer
      A transient reference to the parent container. This allows providers to access shared resources like the executor service.

    • uuid

      private String uuid
      The unique UUID for this specific provider instance. Crucial for distinguishing between multiple instances of the same provider class (e.g., two different Ollama endpoints).

    • baseUrl

      private String baseUrl
      The base URL of the provider's API endpoint.

      This allows the provider to target official cloud services or alternative backends like local proxies, Ollama, or specialized vendor endpoints.

    • folderName

      private String folderName
      An optional custom folder name for this provider's configuration. If null or empty, the uuid is used as the directory name.

    • displayName

      private String displayName
      The user-facing display name for this instance (e.g., 'Groq Cloud').

    • description

      private String description
      The user-facing description of this instance.

    • keysAcquisitionUri

      private String keysAcquisitionUri
      The URI where users can acquire API keys for this provider.

    • tokenizerType

      private TokenizerType tokenizerType
      The type of tokenizer used by this provider. This determines how accurately the Context Window Garbage Collector can estimate the token count before making an API call.

    • apiKeyRequired

      private boolean apiKeyRequired
      Whether this provider requires an API key to function. If false, the ASI will allow requests even if the key pool is empty (e.g. local Ollama).

    • enabled

      private boolean enabled
      Whether this provider is enabled and should be offered to the user.

    • currentKey

      private transient String currentKey
      The API key currently in use by this provider. Captured during key rotation.

    • keyPool

      private volatile List<String> keyPool
      The internal cache of loaded API keys, reloaded from disk on change.

    • round

      private final AtomicInteger round
      Atomic counter for round-robin key selection.

    • models

      private transient List<? extends AbstractModel> models
      Lazy-loaded cache of models discovered via the provider's API.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractAiProvider

      public AbstractAiProvider()
      No-arg constructor required for Kryo serialization and dynamic instantiation.

    • AbstractAiProvider

      public AbstractAiProvider(String uuid)
      Constructs a new provider instance with a specific UUID.
      Parameters:
      uuid - The unique ID for this instance.

  • Method Details

    • listModels

      public abstract List<? extends AbstractModel> listModels()
      Fetches the list of all models available from the provider's API. This method is intended to be called by the caching mechanism.
      Returns:
      A list of provider-specific AbstractModel objects.

    • getProviderId

      public String getProviderId()
      Compatibility alias for getUuid() to maintain integration with existing IDE and UI components that expect a provider ID.
      Returns:
      The unique UUID of this provider instance.

    • getModels

      public List<? extends AbstractModel> getModels()
      Gets the list of models, using a lazy-loaded cache. If the cache is empty, it calls listModels() to populate it. If fetching fails, it returns an empty list and caches it to prevent repeated failures.

      Implementation details: This method automatically filters the results of listModels() against the allowedModels whitelist. If the whitelist is empty, all discovered models are returned.

      Returns:
      The cached list of models.

    • findModel

      public Optional<? extends AbstractModel> findModel(String modelId)
      Finds a single model by its unique ID within this provider.
      Parameters:
      modelId - The ID of the model to find.
      Returns:
      An Optional containing the model if found, otherwise empty.

    • refreshModels

      public List<? extends AbstractModel> refreshModels()
      Clears the local model cache and forces a reload from the API on the next call to getModels().
      Returns:
      The newly fetched list of models.

    • getAllSupportedActions

      public Set<String> getAllSupportedActions()
      Gets a set of all unique supported actions across all models offered by this provider, using the cached model list.
      Returns:
      A set of unique action strings.

    • getKeysAcquisitionUri

      public URI getKeysAcquisitionUri()
      Gets the URI where users can acquire API keys for this provider.
      Returns:
      The acquisition URI, or null if not set.

    • getApiKeyHint

      public abstract String getApiKeyHint()
      Gets a template or hint string to display when the API keys file is empty.
      Returns:
      The API key hint text.

    • hasKeys

      public boolean hasKeys()
      Checks if there are any valid (non-comment, non-empty) API keys configured for this provider.
      Returns:
      true if at least one key exists.

    • reloadKeyPool

      public void reloadKeyPool()
      Reloads the API key pool from the provider's specific configuration file.

      This method is typically invoked when the user manually updates the api_keys.txt file or when a rotation is forced via hokusPocus().

    • getCurrentKey

      public String getCurrentKey()
      Returns the API key currently in use by this provider instance.

      If no key is currently active, this method performs an initial selection from the pool.

      Returns:
      The active API key, or null if the pool is empty.

    • hokusPocus

      public void hokusPocus()
      Resets the internal state of the provider, typically to force a key rotation.

      Implementation details: Subclasses should override this method to nullify their native API clients, ensuring they are reconstructed with the next available key from the pool.

    • getNextKey

      protected String getNextKey()
      Selects the next API key from the pool using a round-robin strategy.

      This method automatically initializes the pool from disk if it hasn't been loaded yet.

      Returns:
      The selected API key, or null if no keys are configured.

    • getProviderDirectory

      public Path getProviderDirectory()
      Resolves the absolute path to the provider's storage directory.

      If a folderName is configured, it is used; otherwise, the directory name defaults to the provider's uuid.

      Returns:
      The path to the provider's configuration and log directory.

    • getKeysFilePath

      public Path getKeysFilePath()
      Resolves the path to the api_keys.txt file for this provider.

      This method ensures the parent directory exists before returning the path.

      Returns:
      The path to the API keys configuration file.

    • ensureKeysFileExists

      public void ensureKeysFileExists()
      Ensures that the api_keys.txt file exists on the host filesystem.

      If the file is missing, a new empty file is created to allow the user to populate it.

    • readApiKeysFile

      private List<String> readApiKeysFile()
      Ensures the API keys file exists on disk, creating it as a completely empty file if missing.
      Returns:
      A list of cleaned, non-empty, non-comment API key strings read from the file.
